ucs.title: [LLM] Moonstone Sleet (Storm-1789) trojanized npm package post-install code execution
ucs.description: Hunts the developer-targeted supply-chain chain Moonstone Sleet uses to compromise software/crypto engineers via malicious npm packages (e.g. harthat-api, harthat-hash, scratchcurrencytoken, node-hide-console-win), where node/npm spawns native LOLBins or PowerShell during install to stage second-stage payloads. This is a near-signature pattern for Storm-1789's developer-recruitment lure as documented by Microsoft Threat Intelligence.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("node.exe","npm.cmd","npm.exe","npx.cmd","yarn.cmd","yarn.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","schtasks.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval suspicious=if(match(process,"(?i)(invoke-webrequest|downloadstring|-enc |frombase64|iex |webclient|net\.webclient|http(s)?:\/\/|appdata|programdata|temp)"),1,0), known_pkg=if(match(parent_process,"(?i)(harthat-api|harthat-hash|node-hide-console-win|scratchcurrencytoken|postinstall|preinstall)"),1,0) | where suspicious=1 OR known_pkg=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("node.exe","npm.cmd","npm.exe","npx.cmd","yarn.cmd","yarn.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","schtasks.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
| extend SuspChild = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-WebRequest","DownloadString","FromBase64","-enc ","-EncodedCommand","IEX ","WebClient","http://","https://","\\AppData\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Temp\\")
| extend KnownPkg = InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("harthat-api","harthat-hash","node-hide-console-win","scratchcurrencytoken","postinstall","preinstall","npm install","npm i ")
| where SuspChild or KnownPkg
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("node.exe","powershell.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe")
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("detankzone","starglowventures","ccwaterfall","blocknovas","matuchok")
      or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Storm-1789 / Moonstone Sleet is one of the very few state actors (alongside Jade Sleet/TraderTraitor) using malicious npm packages to target Web3 and software developers. Legitimate npm installs almost never spawn powershell.exe with -enc, certutil, or schtasks from a postinstall hook. Anchoring on the node/npm parent + suspicious LOLBin child + Storm-1789 package names yields very high fidelity v

ucs.title: [LLM] Moonstone Sleet trojanized PuTTY / DeTankZone game loader chain (SplitLoader / YouieLoader)
ucs.description: Detects the actor's signature lure where a fake-recruiter persona ships a tar.lz / ZIP containing a trojanized PuTTY (IsoBurst) or the DeTankZone / DeTankWar / DeFiTankWar game; once executed from a user-writable path the binary side-loads SplitLoader/YouieLoader, then establishes scheduled-task or Run-key persistence and beacons over HTTPS. The combination of game/PuTTY-named binary launched from Downloads/Temp + immediate persistence is uniquely Moonstone Sleet.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_path) as paths values(Processes.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (match(Processes.process_path,"(?i)\\\\Users\\\\[^\\\\]+\\\\(Downloads|Desktop|AppData\\\\Local\\\\Temp|Public)\\\\") AND Processes.process_name IN ("putty.exe","isoburst.exe","detankzone.exe","detankwar.exe","defitankwar.exe","deltank.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("putty.exe","isoburst.exe","detankzone.exe","detankwar.exe","defitankwar.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as procVariety values(parent_process_name) as parents by host user | where procVariety>=2 OR like(parent_process_name,"%putty%") OR like(parent_process_name,"%tank%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let LureBins = dynamic(["putty.exe","isoburst.exe","detankzone.exe","detankwar.exe","defitankwar.exe","deltank.exe"]);
let UserPaths = dynamic([@"\Users\",@"\Downloads\",@"\Desktop\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\",@"\Users\Public\"]);
let Stage1 =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ (LureBins) and FolderPath has_any (UserPaths)
    | project T1=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, Stage1Proc=FileName, Stage1Path=FolderPath, Stage1Cmd=ProcessCommandLine, Stage1PID=ProcessId;
let Stage2 =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LureBins)
       and FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe")
    | project T2=Timestamp, DeviceId, Stage2Proc=FileName, Stage2Cmd=ProcessCommandLine, InitProc=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let Stage3Persist =
    union
    (DeviceRegistryEvents
     | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
     | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
     | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
     | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LureBins) or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ (LureBins)
     | project T3=Timestamp, DeviceId, PersistType="RunKey", PersistDetail=strcat(RegistryKey,"\\",RegistryValueName," => ",RegistryValueData)),
    (DeviceProcessEvents
     | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
     | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/CREATE")
     | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LureBins) or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ (LureBins)
     | project T3=Timestamp, DeviceId, PersistType="SchedTask", PersistDetail=ProcessCommandLine);
Stage1
| join kind=inner (Stage2) on DeviceId
| where T2 between (T1 .. T1 + 30m)
| join kind=leftouter (Stage3Persist) on DeviceId
| where isnull(T3) or T3 between (T1 .. T1 + 2h)
| project T1, DeviceName, AccountName, Stage1Proc, Stage1Path, Stage1Cmd, Stage2Proc, Stage2Cmd, PersistType, PersistDetail
| order by T1 desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft and Mandiant both attribute the DeTankZone/DeTankWar fake-game and the trojanized PuTTY (IsoBurst) campaigns specifically to Moonstone Sleet / Storm-1789 — these are not shared with broader Lazarus clusters since the 2023 split. Real PuTTY almost never executes from Downloads/Temp and then spawns rundll32/schtasks, and a binary literally named detankzone.exe spawning persistence is essen
